methodsAttempted.notes: Confirmed Wageningen University & Research official web presence under wur.nl and WUR Support under support.wur.nl; robots.txt was checked before fetching.

methodsAttempted.query: site:wur.nl Wageningen University Research generative AI students assessment policy OR guidelines
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search surfaced official WUR Support PDF attachments and repository publications; repository publications were not used as policy evidence.
Firecrawl search located the WUR Support PDF titled Rules and Guidelines for Student Use of GenAI and the Examining Board rules PDF.

methodsAttempted.query: site:wur.nl "Rules and Guidelines for Student Use of GenAI" "Student Charter"
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search located the official WUR Support Student Charter page that links the current 2025-2026 GenAI rules attachment.

methodsAttempted.query: Student Charter 2025-2026 linked GenAI and Examining Board attachments
methodsAttempted.notes: The Student Charter page was used to verify current attachment IDs for the GenAI rules PDF, Examining Board rules PDF, and related official student-support pages.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found public WUR Support and WUR student-support evidence for student GenAI rules, Examining Board fraud treatment of unauthorized ready-made AI content, MSc thesis AI-use conditions, and WUR Library research guidance. The researcher-specific KB page was inaccessible/retired, and a public generative-AI training course page was rejected as low-specificity training content rather than policy evidence.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Student Charter 2025-2026
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The Student Charter 2025-2026 comprises the rights and obligations of Wageningen University students. The codes of conduct table lists Rules and guidelines for student use of GenAI and describes it as a description of the rules and guidelines for student use of GenAI in education.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The Student Charter page frames the charter as student rights and obligations and links the GenAI student rules/guidelines as a code-of-conduct resource.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Summary and Codes of conduct education and research table

artifacts.sourceTitle: Rules and guidelines for student use of GenAI 2025-2026
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Always check your course guide in Brightspace to understand whether, and to what extent, GenAI is permitted in your coursework. If there is no information in your course guide, using GenAI to generate the content of your assignments is not allowed and can be considered as an irregularity or even fraud. This also applies for theses; seek guidance from your lecturer or supervisor.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The GenAI rules require students to check the course guide and say GenAI-generated assignment content is not allowed when the course guide is silent.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: 1. Always check your course guide in Brightspace
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: Rules and guidelines for student use of GenAI 2025-2026
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Rules and guidelines for student use of GenAI 2025-2026
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: You are never allowed to submit work entirely generated by GenAI as your own or fabricate research data. Doing so constitutes academic fraud. When using GenAI tools, be transparent about their role in your work. You are ultimately responsible for the content you submit and for the accuracy and integrity of your work.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The GenAI rules prohibit submitting wholly GenAI-generated work as one's own and require transparency about GenAI's role.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: 3. Maintain academic integrity and be transparent
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Rules and guidelines for student use of GenAI 2025-2026
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Anything that you feed into external GenAI tools like ChatGPT may be stored by the company indefinitely and might be used in unspecified ways. Never put (personal) data of others, information that infringes on intellectual property rights or sensitive or confidential (research) data into a chatbot or any other public GenAI tool.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The GenAI rules warn about external GenAI storage and prohibit entering others' personal data, IP-infringing information, or sensitive/confidential research data into public GenAI tools.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Rules and Regulations of the Examining Boards of Wageningen University
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Any use of artificial intelligence (AI) to create ready-made content of an assignment is considered an irregularity that may lead to suspicion of fraud, unless the use of AI is explicitly allowed in the assignment description and the use of AI has been documented adequately.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The Examining Board rules treat unauthorized ready-made AI assignment content as an irregularity that may lead to suspicion of fraud.

artifacts.sourceTitle: MSc Thesis Course Guide Wageningen University
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: In principle, the use of AI is allowed as/for: Sparring partner / Brainstorming; Feedback tool for textual improvement; Data processing script development; Literature searching; Transcription. Under the conditions that the use of AI may only be in support of the development of these skills and not a replacement of these skills; students remain accountable; and use must be acknowledged or documented.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The MSc thesis guide lists permitted AI use cases and attaches conditions around skill support, accountability, acknowledgement/documentation, data, copyright, literature searching, and code verification.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Artificial intelligence for searching & screening literature | Library guide
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: It is important to always document your use of AI. In addition, do not cite AI as if it were a source or an author. WUR Library urges you to be transparent in your use of AI tools and to always check a publisher's policy before including AI-generated content in your publication. For confidentiality and proprietary reasons, WUR Library advises reviewers never to upload a non-published manuscript into any externally hosted tool.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The WUR Library guide gives research-oriented AI documentation, authorship/source, publisher-policy, and peer-review confidentiality guidance.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Document your use of AI; Publishing and peer review
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow source-status claim because the official Student Charter page directly identifies the GenAI rules resource.
Approved because the official GenAI rules PDF directly states the course-guide permission rule and thesis caveat.
Approved because the official GenAI rules PDF directly supports the transparency and wholly generated work statements.
Approved because the official GenAI rules PDF directly supports the public GenAI tool data-security restriction.
Approved because the official Examining Board rules PDF directly states the ready-made AI content irregularity/fraud language.
Approved as MSc-thesis-scoped research guidance directly supported by the official WUR MSc Thesis Course Guide.
Approved as WUR Library research guidance; claim wording preserves the advisory scope and does not treat it as a university-wide binding policy.

artifacts.title: Wageningen University & Research staged AI policy evidence draft
artifacts.summary: Staged OpenClaw crawl for Wageningen University & Research found official public WUR Support and WUR-branded student-support evidence for current 2025-2026 student GenAI rules, Examining Board treatment of unauthorized ready-made AI content, MSc thesis AI-use conditions, and WUR Library research guidance. The kept claims are scoped to students, Examining Board rules, MSc thesis work, or WUR Library research guidance. A researcher support KB page was inaccessible/retired, and a GenAI training course page was rejected as low-specificity training content.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Claims are staged candidates only; publishAsCanonical and isCanonical are false.
The researcher-specific WUR Support article linked from the Library guide was inaccessible or retired and was not used for a claim.
Repository publications and a public generative-AI training course were not used as policy evidence.
WUR Library guidance was kept in advisory research-guidance language and was not converted into a binding university-wide policy.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
